test.command: pnpm -C packages/shared exec vitest run src/delivery-loop/domain/failure-signature.test.ts src/delivery-loop/store/dispatch-intent-store.test.ts && pnpm -C apps/www exec vitest run src/server-lib/delivery-loop/v3/reducer.test.ts src/server-lib/delivery-loop/v3/process-effects.test.ts src/app/api/daemon-event/route.test.ts && pnpm -C apps/www exec vitest run src/server-lib/e2e.test.ts
test.summary: 446 tests passed across packages/shared and apps/www

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the out-of-order signal detection pattern with configurable strictness modes (strict, lenient, ci-verdict). This pattern is critical for maintaining correct event ordering semantics in the delivery loop and appears in multiple features.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Used in both m2-simplify-reducer-transition-core (unified isOutOfOrderSignal) and m2-enforce-canonical-parity-and-correlation (lane-based routing with isOutOfOrderGateSignal and isOutOfOrderFailureSignal). The lane-based routing (infra vs agent) is a critical behavioral contract for deterministic retry/dead-letter semantics.
